---
title: "applied_kappa"
author: "Alexander Unger"
date: "2026-04-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# packages to install
library(haven)      
library(AER)        
library(sandwich)   
library(lmtest)     
library(boot)
library(cobalt)
library(viridis)
 library(gridExtra)

set.seed(42)  # Reproducibility for bootstrap

data_path <- "/Users/alexung/desktop/suw_jbes_replicate"   
cat("Loading data...\n")
sipp <- read_dta(file.path(data_path, "sipp.dta"))
card <- read_dta(file.path(data_path, "card.dta"))
ae98 <- read_dta(file.path(data_path, "ae98.dta"))



################ functions 

# 1a. Logistic function (inverse logit)
sigmoid <- function(x) 1 / (1 + exp(-x))

# 1b. Logit MLE for instrument propensity score p(X) = P(Z=1|X)
logit_mle <- function(Z, X) {
  df <- data.frame(Z = Z, X)
  fit <- glm(Z ~ . - 1, data = df, family = binomial(link = "logit"))
  fitted.values(fit)
}


#1c. Covariate Balancing Propensity Score (CBPS)
cbps <- function(Z, X, tol = 1e-8, max_iter = 1000) {
  n <- length(Z)
  k <- ncol(X)
  
  # Moment conditions: mean over i of [ (Z_i/p_i - (1-Z_i)/(1-p_i)) * X_i ]
  moment_fn <- function(b) {
    p <- pmin(pmax(sigmoid(X %*% b), 1e-10), 1 - 1e-10)
    residual <- as.vector(Z / p - (1 - Z) / (1 - p))
    colMeans(residual * X)  # k-vector
  }
  
  # Jacobian of moment conditions w.r.t. b
  jac_fn <- function(b) {
    p <- pmin(pmax(sigmoid(X %*% b), 1e-10), 1 - 1e-10)
    # d/db [ Z/p - (1-Z)/(1-p) ] = [-Z(1-p)/p - (1-Z)p/(1-p)] * X
    w <- as.vector(-Z * (1 - p) / p - (1 - Z) * p / (1 - p))
    t(w * X) %*% X / n  # k x k matrix
  }
  
  # Start from logit MLE (warm start for better convergence)
  fit_mle <- glm(Z ~ X - 1, family = binomial(link = "logit"))
  b <- coef(fit_mle)
  
  converged <- FALSE
  for (i in seq_len(max_iter)) {
    m <- moment_fn(b)
    if (max(abs(m)) < tol) { converged <- TRUE; break }
    
    J <- jac_fn(b)
    step <- tryCatch(solve(J, -m), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(step)) break
    
    # Backtracking line search (ensures the step reduces the residual norm)
    alpha <- 1.0
    for (j in seq_len(30)) {
      if (max(abs(moment_fn(b + alpha * step))) < max(abs(m))) break
      alpha <- alpha * 0.5
    }
    b <- b + alpha * step
  }
  
  p <- pmin(pmax(sigmoid(X %*% b), 1e-10), 1 - 1e-10)
  list(p = as.vector(p), converged = converged)
}



# 1d) Kappa weights (Abadie 2003, Lemma 2.1)
kappa_weights <- function(Z, D, p) {
  list(
    kappa  = 1 - D * (1 - Z) / (1 - p) - (1 - D) * Z / p,
    kappa1 = D * (Z - p) / (p * (1 - p)),
    kappa0 = (1 - D) * ((1 - Z) - (1 - p)) / (p * (1 - p))
  )
}



# Normalized Uysal (2011) estimator [RECOMMENDED]
tau_u <- function(Y, Z, D, p) {
  s1 <- sum(Z / p)
  s0 <- sum((1 - Z) / (1 - p))
  numerator   <- sum(Y * Z / p) / s1 - sum(Y * (1 - Z) / (1 - p)) / s0
  denominator <- sum(D * Z / p) / s1 - sum(D * (1 - Z) / (1 - p)) / s0
  numerator / denominator
}

# Normalized Abadie-Cattaneo (2018) estimator
tau_a10 <- function(Y, Z, D, p) {
  kw <- kappa_weights(Z, D, p)
  sum(kw$kappa1 * Y) / sum(kw$kappa1) - sum(kw$kappa0 * Y) / sum(kw$kappa0)
}

# Unnormalized estimator (generic): which = "a", "a1", or "a0"
tau_unnorm <- function(Y, Z, D, p, which = "a") {
  kw <- kappa_weights(Z, D, p)
  numerator <- mean(Y * (Z - p) / (p * (1 - p)))
  denom_val <- switch(which,
    "a"  = mean(kw$kappa),
    "a1" = mean(kw$kappa1),
    "a0" = mean(kw$kappa0)
  )
  numerator / denom_val
}



# 2a. 2SLS with HC1-robust standard errors
run_2sls <- function(Y, D, Z, X_df, endog_name = "D") {
  # Build formula: Y ~ covariates + D | covariates + Z
  cov_names <- names(X_df)[names(X_df) != "(Intercept)"]
  
  df <- data.frame(Y = Y, D = D, Z = Z, X_df)
  names(df)[names(df) == "D"] <- endog_name
  names(df)[names(df) == "Z"] <- "instrument"
  
  if (length(cov_names) == 0) {
    fml <- as.formula(paste("Y ~", endog_name, "| instrument"))
  } else {
    cov_str <- paste(cov_names, collapse = " + ")
    fml <- as.formula(paste("Y ~", endog_name, "+", cov_str,
                            "| instrument +", cov_str))
  }
  
  fit <- ivreg(fml, data = df)
  
  # HC1 robust covariance (matches Stata vce(robust))
  vcov_r <- vcovHC(fit, type = "HC1")
  ct <- coeftest(fit, vcov = vcov_r)
  
  # Return coefficient and SE for the endogenous variable
  list(
    coef = ct[endog_name, "Estimate"],
    se   = ct[endog_name, "Std. Error"],
    pval = ct[endog_name, "Pr(>|t|)"]
  )
}




# 2b. Bootstrap standard errors for all kappa estimators
boot_se_all <- function(Y, Z, D, X_mat, R = 500) {
  n <- length(Y)
  
  # Function to compute all 6 kappa estimators on a (possibly resampled) dataset
  compute_all <- function(Y, Z, D, X) {
    # MLE propensity score
    p_ml <- tryCatch(logit_mle(Z, X), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(p_ml)) return(rep(NA, 6))
    
    # CBPS propensity score
    cb_res <- tryCatch(cbps(Z, X), error = function(e) list(p = NULL, converged = FALSE))
    
    estimates <- numeric(6)
    estimates[1] <- if (!is.null(cb_res$p) && cb_res$converged)
                      tryCatch(tau_u(Y, Z, D, cb_res$p), error = function(e) NA)
                    else NA
    estimates[2] <- tryCatch(tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_ml), error = function(e) NA)
    estimates[3] <- tryCatch(tau_a10(Y, Z, D, p_ml), error = function(e) NA)
    estimates[4] <- tryCatch(tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a"),  error = function(e) NA)
    estimates[5] <- tryCatch(tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a1"), error = function(e) NA)
    estimates[6] <- tryCatch(tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"), error = function(e) NA)
    estimates
  }
  
  # Point estimates
  point <- compute_all(Y, Z, D, X_mat)
  
  # Bootstrap
  boot_mat <- matrix(NA, nrow = R, ncol = 6)
  for (r in seq_len(R)) {
    idx <- sample(n, n, replace = TRUE)
    boot_mat[r, ] <- compute_all(Y[idx], Z[idx], D[idx], X_mat[idx, , drop = FALSE])
  }
  
  ses <- apply(boot_mat, 2, function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  list(
    estimates = setNames(point, c("tau_cb_u","tau_ml_u","tau_ml_a10",
                                   "tau_ml_a","tau_ml_t","tau_ml_a0")),
    se        = setNames(ses,   c("tau_cb_u","tau_ml_u","tau_ml_a10",
                                   "tau_ml_a","tau_ml_t","tau_ml_a0"))
  )
}



# 2c. Format results as "coef (se)" with significance stars
fmt <- function(coef, se, digits = 3) {
  if (is.na(coef)) return("NA")
  pval <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(coef / se))
  stars <- ifelse(pval < 0.01, "***",
           ifelse(pval < 0.05, "**",
           ifelse(pval < 0.10, "*", "")))
  sprintf(paste0("%.", digits, "f%s\n(%.", digits, "f)"),
          round(coef, digits), stars, round(se, digits))
}

```



```{r}
# 3. TABLE 2: ANGRIST (1990) - MILITARY SERVICE
cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("TABLE 2: ANGRIST (1990) - Causal Effects of Military Service\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")

# --- Data preparation ---
sipp_clean <- sipp[sipp$rsncode != 999 & !is.na(sipp$educ) & !is.na(sipp$kwage), ]
sipp_clean$age  <- sipp_clean$age_5
sipp_clean$age2 <- sipp_clean$age_5^2
sipp_clean$age3 <- sipp_clean$age_5^3
sipp_clean$lwage     <- log(sipp_clean$kwage)          # log wages in dollars
sipp_clean$lwage_cnt <- log(100 * sipp_clean$kwage)    # log wages in cents
cat(sprintf("N = %d (should be 3,027)\n\n", nrow(sipp_clean)))

# --- Three covariate specifications ---
# Spec 1: Linear age (Table 2 columns 1-2)
X1 <- model.matrix(~ age, data = sipp_clean)

# Spec 2: Cubic polynomial in age (Table 2 columns 3-4)
X2 <- model.matrix(~ age + age2 + age3, data = sipp_clean)

# Spec 3: Saturated - indicator for each age value (Table 2 columns 5-6)
# This fully controls for age nonparametrically
sipp_clean$age_fac <- factor(sipp_clean$age_5)
X3 <- model.matrix(~ age_fac, data = sipp_clean)

specs <- list(
  list(X = X1, name = "Age linear",   X_df = data.frame(age = sipp_clean$age)),
  list(X = X2, name = "Cubic in age", X_df = data.frame(age  = sipp_clean$age,
                                                          age2 = sipp_clean$age2,
                                                          age3 = sipp_clean$age3)),
  list(X = X3, name = "Saturated age",X_df = data.frame(age_fac = sipp_clean$age_fac))
)

# Run for both outcome measurements (cents and dollars)
for (spec in specs) {
  cat(sprintf("--- Specification: %s ---\n", spec$name))
  for (outcome in c("lwage_cnt", "lwage")) {
    Y <- sipp_clean[[outcome]]
    D <- sipp_clean$nvstat
    Z <- sipp_clean$rsncode

    # 2SLS
    iv_res <- run_2sls(Y, D, Z, spec$X_df, endog_name = "nvstat")

    # Kappa estimators with bootstrap SE (use 200 reps here for speed;
    # increase to 500 for final results matching the paper)
    kappa_res <- boot_se_all(Y, Z, D, spec$X, R = 200)

    cat(sprintf("  Outcome: %s\n", outcome))
    cat(sprintf("    2SLS:       %s\n", fmt(iv_res$coef, iv_res$se)))
    cat(sprintf("    tau_cb_u:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_cb_u"],
                                            kappa_res$se["tau_cb_u"])))
    cat(sprintf("    tau_ml_u:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_u"],
                                            kappa_res$se["tau_ml_u"])))
    cat(sprintf("    tau_ml_a10: %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_a10"],
                                            kappa_res$se["tau_ml_a10"])))
    cat(sprintf("    tau_ml_a:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_a"],
                                            kappa_res$se["tau_ml_a"])))
    cat(sprintf("    tau_ml_t:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_t"],
                                            kappa_res$se["tau_ml_t"])))
    cat(sprintf("    tau_ml_a0:  %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_a0"],
                                            kappa_res$se["tau_ml_a0"])))
    cat("\n")
  }
}




```



```{r}
# 4. TABLE 3: CARD (1995) - COLLEGE EDUCATION

cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("TABLE 3: CARD (1995) - Causal Effects of College Education\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")

# --- Data preparation ---
card_clean <- card
# Drop existing lwage (measured in log cents) and recompute:
#   lwage     = ln(wage/100)  [dollars]
#   lwage_cnt = ln(wage)      [cents, since wage is already in cents]
card_clean$lwage     <- log(card_clean$wage / 100)
card_clean$lwage_cnt <- log(card_clean$wage)
card_clean$somecol   <- as.numeric(card_clean$educ > 12)   # some college
card_clean$educ16    <- as.numeric(card_clean$educ >= 16)  # college completion
cat(sprintf("N = %d (should be 3,010)\n\n", nrow(card_clean)))

# Card (1995) specification: full set of controls
cov_card <- c("black","south","smsa","smsa66",
              "reg661","reg662","reg663","reg664","reg665","reg666","reg667","reg668",
              "exper","expersq")
X_card <- model.matrix(~ black + south + smsa + smsa66 +
                          reg661 + reg662 + reg663 + reg664 + reg665 +
                          reg666 + reg667 + reg668 + exper + expersq,
                        data = card_clean)
X_card_df <- card_clean[, cov_card]

# Kitagawa (2015) specification: more parsimonious
cov_kita <- c("smsa","smsa66","black","south","south66")
X_kita <- model.matrix(~ smsa + smsa66 + black + south + south66, data = card_clean)
X_kita_df <- card_clean[, cov_kita]

treatments <- list(
  list(D = card_clean$somecol, name = "Some college (educ>12)"),
  list(D = card_clean$educ16,  name = "College completion (educ>=16)")
)

for (tr in treatments) {
  cat(sprintf("=== Treatment: %s ===\n", tr$name))
  for (spec in list(list(X=X_card, Xdf=X_card_df, name="Card spec"),
                    list(X=X_kita, Xdf=X_kita_df, name="Kitagawa spec"))) {
    cat(sprintf("  -- %s --\n", spec$name))
    for (outcome in c("lwage_cnt", "lwage")) {
      Y <- card_clean[[outcome]]
      D <- tr$D
      Z <- card_clean$nearc4

      iv_res    <- run_2sls(Y, D, Z, spec$Xdf, endog_name = "D")
      kappa_res <- boot_se_all(Y, Z, D, spec$X, R = 200)

      cat(sprintf("    Outcome: %s\n", outcome))
      cat(sprintf("      2SLS:       %s\n", fmt(iv_res$coef, iv_res$se)))
      cat(sprintf("      tau_cb_u:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_cb_u"],
                                                 kappa_res$se["tau_cb_u"])))
      cat(sprintf("      tau_ml_u:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_u"],
                                                 kappa_res$se["tau_ml_u"])))
      cat(sprintf("      tau_ml_a10: %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_a10"],
                                                 kappa_res$se["tau_ml_a10"])))
      cat(sprintf("      tau_ml_a:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_a"],
                                                 kappa_res$se["tau_ml_a"])))
      cat(sprintf("      tau_ml_t:   %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_t"],
                                                 kappa_res$se["tau_ml_t"])))
      cat(sprintf("      tau_ml_a0:  %s\n", fmt(kappa_res$estimates["tau_ml_a0"],
                                                 kappa_res$se["tau_ml_a0"])))
      cat("\n")
    }
  }
}


```






```{r}
# EXTENSION: OUTCOME WEIGHTS ANALYSIS (Knaus 2024)

if (!require("OutcomeWeights")) install.packages("OutcomeWeights", dependencies = TRUE)
if (!require("ggplot2"))        install.packages("ggplot2",        dependencies = TRUE)

library(OutcomeWeights)   # dml_with_smoother(), get_outcome_weights(), plot()
library(ggplot2)


kappa_outcome_weights <- function(Y, Z, D, p) {
  n  <- length(Y)
  kw <- kappa_weights(Z, D, p)   # from Section 1d of main script

  # tau_u (Uysal): normalized ratio estimator
  s1  <- sum(Z / p);  s0 <- sum((1 - Z) / (1 - p))
  dD  <- sum(D * Z / p) / s1 - sum(D * (1 - Z) / (1 - p)) / s0
  w_u <- (Z / p / s1 - (1 - Z) / (1 - p) / s0) / dD

  # tau_a10 (Abadie-Cattaneo): normalized kappa ratio
  w_a10 <- kw$kappa1 / sum(kw$kappa1) - kw$kappa0 / sum(kw$kappa0)

  # unnormalized: common numerator structure, different denominators
  num_w <- (Z - p) / (p * (1 - p)) / n
  w_a   <- num_w / mean(kw$kappa)
  w_a1  <- num_w / mean(kw$kappa1)
  w_a0  <- num_w / mean(kw$kappa0)

  # Verify tau = w'Y for all estimators
  stopifnot(
    abs(sum(w_u   * Y) - tau_u(Y, Z, D, p))           < 1e-8,
    abs(sum(w_a10 * Y) - tau_a10(Y, Z, D, p))         < 1e-8,
    abs(sum(w_a   * Y) - tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p, "a")) < 1e-8,
    abs(sum(w_a1  * Y) - tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p, "a1"))< 1e-8,
    abs(sum(w_a0  * Y) - tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p, "a0"))< 1e-8
  )

  list(w_u = as.vector(w_u), w_a10 = as.vector(w_a10),
       w_a = as.vector(w_a), w_a1  = as.vector(w_a1),
       w_a0 = as.vector(w_a0))
}


weight_diag_table <- function(w_list, names_vec) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(w_list), function(i) {
    w <- w_list[[i]]
    data.frame(
      Estimator  = names_vec[i],
      Sum_w      = round(sum(w), 6),        # 0 = translation invariant
      ESS        = round(1 / sum(w^2), 0),  # effective sample size
      Pct_neg    = round(mean(w < 0) * 100, 1),
      Max_abs_w  = round(max(abs(w)), 6),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
}



```

```{r}

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 5: ANGRIST (1990) — Military Service
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("SECTION 5: ANGRIST (1990) — Military Service\n")
cat("Z = draft eligibility | D = veteran | Y = log wages (dollars)\n")
cat("Covariate spec: linear age\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

# --- Data (already prepared in Section 3 of main script) ---
Y5 <- sipp_clean$lwage
D5 <- sipp_clean$nvstat
Z5 <- sipp_clean$rsncode
# X for DML must have NO intercept (OutcomeWeights convention, like Knaus)
X5 <- as.data.frame(model.matrix(~ 0 + age + I(age^2) + I(age^3), data = sipp_clean))

# --- 5a. DML via OutcomeWeights (Knaus 2024) ---
cat("--- 5a. Double ML (Knaus 2024) ---\n")
cat("Running dml_with_smoother() with 5-fold cross-fitting...\n")
dml_5 <- dml_with_smoother(Y5, D5, X5, Z5, n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
# Print ONLY the IV-relevant rows
res_dml5 <- summary(dml_5)
cat("DML results (IV-relevant estimators only):\n")
print(res_dml5[c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"), , drop = FALSE])

# Extract DML outcome weights: w_i s.t. tau_DML = sum(w_i * Y_i)
# get_outcome_weights() returns an NxK matrix (one column per estimator)
# Extract outcome weights: omega is a (4 x N) matrix
omega_5      <- get_outcome_weights(dml_5)
w_plriv5     <- as.vector(omega_5$omega["PLR-IV",    ])
w_waldaipw5  <- as.vector(omega_5$omega["Wald-AIPW", ])
# omega_5$omega rows: PLR, PLR-IV, AIPW-ATE, Wald-AIPW
# Verify: omega_5$omega %*% Y == point estimates
cat("\nVerify omega'Y = point estimates:",
    all.equal(as.numeric(omega_5$omega %*% Y5),
              as.numeric(summary(dml_5)[, 1])), "\n\n")



# Algebraic verification (Knaus 2024, core claim):
# The weights are EXACT — tau_hat = sum(omega_i * Y_i) to machine precision.
# This is not an approximation: it is an algebraic identity derived from the
# smoother matrices. If TRUE, the Love plots and ESS diagnostics are valid.
cat("\nVerify omega'Y = DML point estimates (should be TRUE):\n")
cat("  PLR-IV:   ", all.equal(sum(w_plriv5   * Y5), res_dml5["PLR-IV",    1]), "\n")
cat("  Wald-AIPW:", all.equal(sum(w_waldaipw5 * Y5), res_dml5["Wald-AIPW", 1]), "\n\n")



# Plot DML weight distributions (Knaus's plot method)
cat("--- DML weight distributions (Knaus plot) ---\n")
plot(dml_5)

# --- 5b. Kappa outcome weights (SUW 2025) ---
cat("\n--- 5b. Kappa estimators (SUW 2025) ---\n")
X5_kappa <- model.matrix(~ age + I(age^2) + I(age^3), data = sipp_clean)
# Note: logit_mle() uses all columns of X5_kappa including the intercept column.
p_ml5 <- logit_mle(Z5, X5_kappa)
kw5   <- kappa_outcome_weights(Y5, Z5, D5, p_ml5)

cat("Point estimates:\n")
cat(sprintf("  tau_u    = %.4f\n", tau_u(Y5, Z5, D5, p_ml5)))
cat(sprintf("  tau_a10  = %.4f\n", tau_a10(Y5, Z5, D5, p_ml5)))
cat(sprintf("  tau_a    = %.4f\n", tau_unnorm(Y5, Z5, D5, p_ml5, "a")))
cat(sprintf("  tau_a1   = %.4f\n", tau_unnorm(Y5, Z5, D5, p_ml5, "a1")))

cat("\nWeight diagnostics (sum(w)=0 iff translation invariant):\n")
diag5 <- weight_diag_table(
  kw5,
  c("tau_u (norm.)", "tau_a10 (norm.)", "tau_a (unnorm.)", "tau_a1 (unnorm.)", "tau_a0 (unnorm.)")
)
print(diag5, row.names = FALSE)

# Add DML Wald-AIPW weights to comparison
diag5_dml <- rbind(
  data.frame(
    Estimator = "PLR-IV (DML)",
    Sum_w     = round(sum(w_plriv5), 6),
    ESS       = round(1 / sum(w_plriv5^2), 0),
    Pct_neg   = round(mean(w_plriv5 < 0) * 100, 1),
    Max_abs_w = round(max(abs(w_plriv5)), 6)
  ),
  data.frame(
    Estimator = "Wald-AIPW (DML)",
    Sum_w     = round(sum(w_waldaipw5), 6),
    ESS       = round(1 / sum(w_waldaipw5^2), 0),
    Pct_neg   = round(mean(w_waldaipw5 < 0) * 100, 1),
    Max_abs_w = round(max(abs(w_waldaipw5)), 6)
  )
)

cat("\nFor comparison, DML Wald-AIPW:\n")
print(diag5_dml, row.names = FALSE)



```

```{r}
# checking the love plots
# cat("\n--- Love plots: covariate balance via outcome weights ---\n")
# X5_bal <- as.data.frame(X5)  # age, age^2, age^3
# love.plot(
#   D5 ~ as.matrix(X5_bal),
#   weights = list(
#     "tau_u (kappa)"       = kw5$w_u    * (2*D5 - 1),
#     "tau_a10 (kappa)"     = kw5$w_a10  * (2*D5 - 1),
#     "PLR-IV (DML)"        = w_plriv5   * (2*D5 - 1),
#     "Wald-AIPW (DML)"     = w_waldaipw5 * (2*D5 - 1)
#   ),
#   thresholds = c(m = 0.1),   # |SMD| <= 0.1 is the conventional "good balance" threshold
#   title      = "Angrist (1990): Covariate Balance by Estimator",
#   var.order  = "unadjusted",
#   binary     = "std",
#   abs        = TRUE,
#   line       = TRUE
# )

library(cobalt)
library(viridis)

threshold = 0.1

# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 5: ANGRIST (1990) — Love plots
# ==============================================================================

create_love_plot_5 = function(title, weights) {
  love.plot(
    D5 ~ as.matrix(X5),
    weights    = weights * (2*D5 - 1),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title,
    thresholds = c(m = threshold),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis(2),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle")
  )
}

love_plot_5_plriv    = create_love_plot_5("Angrist (1990): PLR-IV",    w_plriv5)
love_plot_5_waldaipw = create_love_plot_5("Angrist (1990): Wald-AIPW", w_waldaipw5)
love_plot_5_tauu     = create_love_plot_5("Angrist (1990): tau_u",     kw5$w_u)
love_plot_5_taua10   = create_love_plot_5("Angrist (1990): tau_a10",   kw5$w_a10)

# Combined figure
grid.arrange(love_plot_5_plriv, love_plot_5_waldaipw,
             love_plot_5_tauu,  love_plot_5_taua10,
             nrow = 2)

```


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SECTION 6: CARD (1995) — College Education
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("SECTION 6: CARD (1995) — College Education\n")
cat("Z = near 4-year college | D = some college (educ>12) | Y = log wages (dollars)\n")
cat("Covariate spec: Card (1995) full controls\n")
cat("WARNING: weak instrument — IV estimates will be imprecise and potentially large\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

# --- Data (already prepared in Section 4 of main script) ---
Y6 <- card_clean$lwage
D6 <- card_clean$somecol
Z6 <- card_clean$nearc4
# X for DML: no intercept
X6 <- as.data.frame(model.matrix(~ 0 + black + south + smsa + smsa66 +
                     reg661 + reg662 + reg663 + reg664 + reg665 +
                     reg666 + reg667 + reg668 + exper + expersq,
                   data = card_clean))

# --- 6a. DML ---
cat("--- 6a. Double ML (Knaus 2024) ---\n")
cat("Running dml_with_smoother() with 5-fold cross-fitting...\n")
dml_6    <- dml_with_smoother(Y6, D6, X6, Z6, n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
cat("\nDML results:\n")
res_dml6 <- summary(dml_6)

# Print only the IV-relevant estimators
cat("DML results (IV-relevant only — PLR and AIPW-ATE are not IV estimators):\n")
print(res_dml6[c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"), , drop = FALSE])

# Extract weights BY NAME — never by position (position can change with package updates)
omega_6     <- get_outcome_weights(dml_6)
w_plriv6    <- as.vector(omega_6$omega["PLR-IV",    ])
w_waldaipw6 <- as.vector(omega_6$omega["Wald-AIPW", ])


plot(dml_6)

# Algebraic verification: tau_hat = sum(omega_i * Y_i) exactly
# This confirms the weights are correct for use in Love plots and diagnostics
cat("\nVerify omega'Y = DML point estimates (should be TRUE):\n")
cat("  PLR-IV:   ", all.equal(sum(w_plriv6    * Y6), res_dml6["PLR-IV",    1]), "\n")
cat("  Wald-AIPW:", all.equal(sum(w_waldaipw6 * Y6), res_dml6["Wald-AIPW", 1]), "\n\n")



# --- 6b. Kappa weights ---
cat("\n--- 6b. Kappa estimators (SUW 2025) ---\n")
p_ml6 <- logit_mle(Z6, X_card)   # X_card has intercept, from main script
kw6   <- kappa_outcome_weights(Y6, Z6, D6, p_ml6)

cat("Point estimates:\n")
cat(sprintf("  tau_u    = %.4f\n", tau_u(Y6, Z6, D6, p_ml6)))
cat(sprintf("  tau_a10  = %.4f\n", tau_a10(Y6, Z6, D6, p_ml6)))
cat(sprintf("  tau_a    = %.4f\n", tau_unnorm(Y6, Z6, D6, p_ml6, "a")))
cat(sprintf("  tau_a1   = %.4f\n", tau_unnorm(Y6, Z6, D6, p_ml6, "a1")))

diag6_kappa <- weight_diag_table(
  kw6,
  c("tau_u (norm.)", "tau_a10 (norm.)",
    "tau_a (unnorm.)", "tau_a1 (unnorm.)", "tau_a0 (unnorm.)")
)

diag6_dml <- rbind(
  data.frame(
    Estimator = "PLR-IV (DML)",
    Sum_w     = round(sum(w_plriv6), 6),
    ESS       = round(1 / sum(w_plriv6^2), 0),
    Pct_neg   = round(mean(w_plriv6 < 0) * 100, 1),
    Max_abs_w = round(max(abs(w_plriv6)), 6)
  ),
  data.frame(
    Estimator = "Wald-AIPW (DML)",
    Sum_w     = round(sum(w_waldaipw6), 6),
    ESS       = round(1 / sum(w_waldaipw6^2), 0),
    Pct_neg   = round(mean(w_waldaipw6 < 0) * 100, 1),
    Max_abs_w = round(max(abs(w_waldaipw6)), 6)
  )
)
cat("\nWeight diagnostics (all estimators combined):\n")
print(rbind(diag6_kappa, diag6_dml), row.names = FALSE)

```




```{r}
# love plot 


create_love_plot_6 = function(title, weights) {
  love.plot(
    D6 ~ as.matrix(X6),
    weights    = weights * (2*D6 - 1),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title,
    thresholds = c(m = threshold),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis(2),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle")
  )
}

love_plot_6_plriv    = create_love_plot_6("Card (1995): PLR-IV",    w_plriv6)
love_plot_6_waldaipw = create_love_plot_6("Card (1995): Wald-AIPW", w_waldaipw6)
love_plot_6_tauu     = create_love_plot_6("Card (1995): tau_u",     kw6$w_u)
love_plot_6_taua10   = create_love_plot_6("Card (1995): tau_a10",   kw6$w_a10)

# Combined figure
grid.arrange(love_plot_6_plriv, love_plot_6_waldaipw,
             love_plot_6_tauu,  love_plot_6_taua10,
             nrow = 2)

love_plot_6_tauu
```
